info.name: Dehasoft Case API
info.description: Laravel 12 e-commerce API for the Dehasoft case study. JWT authentication, multi-currency display backed by daily TCMB exchange rates, and a transactional checkout flow with admin-gated order completion.

request.description:
Liveness / readiness probe. Returns the per-dependency state of the
database and redis. The HTTP status is 200 when every dependency
answers, otherwise 503 with the failing services flagged as `down`.
Create a new user account and immediately issue a JWT.

response.name: email already taken

request.description: Verify credentials and return a fresh JWT.

response.name: rate limit exceeded

request.description: Blacklist the current bearer token so it cannot be reused.

Rotate the current bearer token. The previous token is blacklisted
and a brand-new one is returned alongside the user payload.
Return the user identified by the bearer token.

Paginated catalogue. Each product carries `prices` in TRY/USD/EUR
computed from the latest cached TCMB rates.
Fetch a single product with its multi-currency price block.

response.body:
{
  "status": "success",
  "message": null,
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Türk Kahvesi 250g",
    "description": "Geleneksel Türk kahvesi, taze çekilmiş.",
    "stock": 80,
    "base_currency": "TRY",
    "price": "95.00",
    "prices": {"TRY": "95.00", "USD": "2.10", "EUR": "1.79"},
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T18:00:00+00:00",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T18:00:00+00:00"
  }
}

request.description: Add a new product to the catalogue. Requires an admin bearer token.

Patch any subset of fields on an existing product. Fields you omit
stay untouched.

Remove a product from the catalogue. Cart entries referencing it
are removed via cascade; existing order_item snapshots survive
unchanged (FK is SET NULL on the order_items side).

Return the authenticated user's cart with each line's per-currency
subtotal and aggregated totals. The cart is created lazily on the
first call so a brand-new account always sees an empty cart shape.

Drop every line from the cart. Idempotent — running it on an
already-empty cart still returns 204.

Push a product onto the cart. If the product is already in the
cart, the quantity is summed with the existing line. The combined
quantity must not exceed the product's stock.

response.name: cumulative quantity exceeds stock

variable.description: Product id of the existing line.

request.description:
Set an existing cart line to an absolute quantity. Use DELETE to
remove the item entirely; setting quantity to 0 is rejected.

response.name: product not in cart

variable.description: Product id of the line to remove.

request.description: Drop a single line from the cart.

response.name: product not in cart

request.description:
Paginated list of the authenticated user's orders, newest first.
Other users' orders are never visible.

Atomic checkout. Reads the cart with FOR UPDATE locks, snapshots
each line into order_items, decrements stock on the referenced
products, persists the order with status PENDING, and clears the
cart — all in one DB transaction. The order's `total_amount` is
stored in TRY (canonical); display currencies are computed live
via the cached TCMB rates.

response.name: empty cart
stock changed since cart-add

request.description:
Fetch one order belonging to the authenticated user. Cross-user
lookups return 404 (existence is deliberately undisclosed).

Cancel a PENDING order owned by the authenticated user. Stock is
returned to every line's referenced product (lines whose product
was deleted are skipped — those stock units stay where they are).
Already-completed or already-cancelled orders return 422.

response.name: terminal state

request.description:
Move a PENDING order to COMPLETED. Stock has already been
decremented at order-creation time so completion is purely a
status mutation. Requires an admin bearer token.

response.name: terminal state
